examples.title: Factorial Calculation
examples.description: Calculate factorial using recursion and iteration
examples.explanation: Two approaches: iterative multiplies 1×2×3×...×n, recursive multiplies n×(n-1)! until reaching base case 1.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for iterative, O(n) for recursive

examples.title: Permutation P(n,r) Calculation
examples.description: Calculate number of permutations using factorial formula
examples.explanation: Two methods: using factorial formula or direct multiplication. Both give P(n,r) = n!/(n-r)! for ordered arrangements.

examples.title: Combination C(n,r) Calculation
examples.description: Calculate number of combinations using factorial and Pascal's identity
examples.explanation: Two approaches: direct factorial formula and recursive Pascal's identity (dynamic programming). C(n,r) counts unordered selections.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for factorial, O(2^n) for naive Pascal, O(n*r) optimized

examples.title: Generate All Permutations
examples.code:
def generate_permutations(arr):
    result = []
    
    def backtrack(start):
        if start == len(arr):
            result.append(arr[:])
            return
        for i in range(start, len(arr)):
            arr[start], arr[i] = arr[i], arr[start]  # swap
            backtrack(start + 1)
            arr[start], arr[i] = arr[i], arr[start]  # swap back
    
    backtrack(0)
    return result

# Test: permutations of [1,2,3]
perms = generate_permutations([1, 2, 3])
for p in perms:
    print(p)
print(f"Total: {len(perms)} (should be 3! = 6)")
examples.explanation: Uses recursive backtracking to generate all permutations. Time complexity O(n×n!) as there are n! permutations each of length n.
